item.title: Father’s rugby journey inspires 21-year-old Amy Peters
item.description: 21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship. While speaking with fijivillage News Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.
item.content:encoded:
21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship.

While speaking with fijivillage News Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.

She says her father’s involvement in rugby played a major role in developing her passion for the game.

Inse rt: Peters sharing about her father 7th July 26

Peters says her father played for Tailevu and Suva and was also believed to have been part of an extended Fiji squad.

The 21-year-old has been part of the Suva Women’s team since 2021.

Speaking about preparations for the upcoming tournament, Peters says the team has been working hard over the past few weeks, with a strong focus on improving their techniques.

She says the coaches have also played an important role in helping the players improve, and she believes preparations are progressing well.

On discipline, Peters says it comes down to how players prepare themselves for a match.

She says if players respect themselves and the game, they will also respect other players on the field.

Peters says the team has not faced many major challenges and thanked Suva supporters for their continued backing.

She hopes supporters will turn up at the ground and get behind the Suva Women’s team during the tournament.

On the women’s side, two Marama Championship matches will be played on Friday.

while Suva faces Lautoka at Bidesi Park for the Bosco Trophy, Nadi will take on Nasinu at King Charles Park, with both matches kicking off at 11am.

Meanwhile, two key Skipper Cup matches will be played on Saturday, with Vatukoula taking on Nadroga at Theodore Park and Tailevu facing Naitasiri at Ratu Cakobau Park.

Both senior matches kick off at 3pm, while the Under-20 matches will start at 1pm.

In Saturday’s Marama Championship action, Namosi will face Nadroga at Thompson Park in Navua at 1pm.

Kadavu will take on Yasawa at Lawaqa Park, while Tailevu faces Naitasiri in Nausori, with both matches kicking off at 11am.

item.title: Defending champions Argentina beat Egypt 3-2 after stunning comeback
item.description: Defending champions Argentina have beaten Egypt 3-2 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash after staging a stunning comeback. Egypt had taken a 2-0 lead before Argentina fought their way back into the match.
item.content:encoded:
Defending champions Argentina have beaten Egypt 3-2 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash after staging a stunning comeback.

Egypt had taken a 2-0 lead before Argentina fought their way back into the match.

It is a heartbreaking loss for Egypt as they nearly pulled it off.

Defender Yasser Ibrahim scored for Egypt, while another Egyptian goal in the 58th minute was ruled out for a foul.

However, Mostafa Zico scored shortly afterwards to extend Egypt’s lead.

Argentina centre-back Cristian Romero pulled one back in the 79th minute after an assist from Lionel Messi.

Messi, who had earlier missed a penalty, scored in the 83rd minute to level the scores at 2-2.

Enzo Fernández then scored a goal to complete the comeback and secure a 3-2 victory.

Argentina have booked their place in the quarter-finals, where they will face either Switzerland or Colombia.

item.title: Queensland Maroons and NSW Blues locked in for State of Origin decider at 10.05pm tonight
item.description: The Queensland Maroons and the New South Wales Blues will go into tonight's State of Origin decider unchanged, with the series set to be decided before a sell-out crowd at Suncorp Stadium . The Blues have been boosted by the availability of Ethan Strange , who has recovered from an ankle injury after initially being placed in a protective boot during camp.
item.content:encoded:
The Queensland Maroons and the New South Wales Blues will go into tonight's State of Origin decider unchanged, with the series set to be decided before a sell-out crowd at Suncorp Stadium .

The Blues have been boosted by the availability of Ethan Strange , who has recovered from an ankle injury after initially being placed in a protective boot during camp.

His recovery allowed Matt Burton to return to Sydney after joining the squad as injury cover.

Queensland have also received good news, with forward Pat Carrigan declared fit despite recently recovering from ankle surgery.

The Maroons will be looking to repeat last year's comeback after losing Game One before winning the final two matches to claim the series.

Halfback Sam Walker has impressed since making his Origin debut and will again partner Cameron Munster , while Selwyn Cobbo , Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow and Kalyn Ponga provide Queensland with plenty of attacking firepower.

For New South Wales, debutant Jack Bostock comes into the side alongside the returning Liam Martin , while much of the focus will be on halfback Nathan Cleary , who is chasing his first State of Origin series-deciding victory.

Game 3 of the 2026 Men's State of Origin kicks off tonight at 10.05pm at Suncorp Stadium in Australia.

item.title: Flying Fijians eye repeat of historic England upset
Saint John's College U18 Raluve team eyes trophy for Levuka
item.description: The Saint John's College Under-18 Raluve team is determined to take the Weet-Bix Raluve trophy to Levuka this year. Expressing his confidence in his side, Head coach Yabaki Driso says the team is focused on taking one game at a time.
item.content:encoded:
The Saint John's College Under-18 Raluve team is determined to take the Weet-Bix Raluve trophy to Levuka this year.

Expressing his confidence in his side, Head coach Yabaki Driso says the team is focused on taking one game at a time.

Driso says the depth and quality of the squad gives them a strong chance of bringing glory to Cawaci this season.

The Ba man says the players have developed a strong understanding after playing together over the years, and their combination has been working well this season.

Reflecting on last week's 27-17 defeat to a strong Dawasamu side, Driso said the team has reviewed its performance and identified areas that need improvement.

He added that Saint John's College has secured a place in the Weet Bix Eastern Zone quarter-finals, which will be played this week, and the team is ready to face whoever they are drawn against.

The fixtures for this week's quarter-finals are expected to be released later tonight.

item.title: Argentina and Egypt clash for quarter-final spot
item.description: Two of world football's biggest stars will go head-to-head tomorrow morning when Lionel Messi's Argentina take on Mohamed Salah's Egypt in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash. The battle between two of the game's greatest forwards headlines a knockout encounter with a place in the quarter-finals at stake.
item.content:encoded:
Two of world football's biggest stars will go head-to-head tomorrow morning when Lionel Messi's Argentina take on Mohamed Salah's Egypt in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash.

The battle between two of the game's greatest forwards headlines a knockout encounter with a place in the quarter-finals at stake.

Defending champions Argentina have looked among the tournament favourites after topping Group J with a perfect record of three wins before surviving a stern test from Cape Verde in the Round of 32, eventually prevailing 3-2 after extra time.

Messi has been at the heart of Argentina's campaign, scoring in all four of their matches so far.

The eight-time Ballon d'Or winner is tied with Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland on seven goals in the race for the Golden Boot.

Egypt, meanwhile, are enjoying one of the best World Cup campaigns in their history.

After finishing second in Group G, the Pharaohs defeated Australia in a penalty shootout to reach the Round of 16 and are now aiming to qualify for the quarter-finals for the first time.

Liverpool star Salah remains Egypt's biggest attacking threat and will be relied upon to unlock Argentina's defence despite battling through a hamstring concern in the previous round.

Argentina will take on Egypt at 4am tomorrow, while Switzerland will face Colombia at 8am in the last FIFA World Cup Round of 16 match.

The winners will become the final two teams to qualify for the quarter-finals, with those matches set to be played on Sunday at 1pm.

Meanwhile, the quarter-finals get underway on Friday when France take on Morocco at 8am, while Spain will face Belgium at 7am on Saturday.

On Sunday, Norway will take on England at 9am.

item.title: Yeshnil Karan on track for Glasgow Commonwealth Games after injury recovery
item.description: The Athletics Association says his latest performance at the Gold Coast Marathon 5km race on Saturday provided another encouraging sign of his progress. They say Karan clocked 15:00 to finish 11th overall in a quality field, running confidently with the lead pack and covering the opening 3,000 metres in a promising 8:43.
item.content:encoded:
The Athletics Association says his latest performance at the Gold Coast Marathon 5km race on Saturday provided another encouraging sign of his progress.

They say Karan clocked 15:00 to finish 11th overall in a quality field, running confidently with the lead pack and covering the opening 3,000 metres in a promising 8:43.

While he faded slightly over the closing stages, the performance reflected the continued rebuilding of his race-specific endurance and finishing speed.

Coach Atma Maharaj says the result confirmed Karan's recovery remained ahead of schedule.

Maharaj says considering where Karan was just six weeks ago, the progress has been extremely encouraging, and the strong aerobic base he developed before surgery has allowed him to recover remarkably well.

He stressed that there is still important work to do, particularly in rebuilding speed endurance and race sharpness, but he is firmly back on track for Glasgow.

Karan returned to Brisbane four weeks ago, where he is training alongside several of Australia's leading middle and long-distance athletes as he completes the final phase of his Commonwealth Games preparation.

Athletics Fiji says with less than a month remaining before the Games, they are encouraged by Karan's continued progress and remains confident he is building towards peak condition ahead of representing Fiji on the international stage in Glasgow.

item.title: Eseta Waqaira replaces injured Kalesi Tawake in Fiji Pearls squad
item.description: Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney. In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.
item.content:encoded:
Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney.

In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.

Waqaira now joins the Fiji Pearls as they continue preparations for the Spirit Series in Sydney.

The Association has wished Waqaira all the best as she joins the Fiji Pearls, expressing confidence that she will embrace the opportunity and give her best in the upcoming Spirit Series.

The Navolau, Naitasiri player, plays goal defence and goal keeper.

item.title: Belgium thrash USA 4-1 to book World Cup quarter-final against Spain
item.description: Belgium have thrashed co-hosts the USA 4-1 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash to book a quarter-final showdown with Spain.C

item.title: De Ketelaere double gives Belgium 2-1 advantage over USA at halftime
item.description: Belgium head into the halftime break with a 2-1 lead over the United States in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash in Seattle S

item.description: Mikel Merino scored the only goal of the match in the 91st minute as Spain beat Portugal 1-0 to secure their place in the FIFA Wor

item.title: Vakatawa, Tagi step in as Radradra and Hoyt injured
item.description: Former French international rugby player Virimi Vakatawa and Bayon-based prop Luke Tagi will join the FIJI Water Flying Fijians ca

item.title: Nasinu Secondary dominate Deans and Raluve round 5 competition
item.description: After Round 5 of the Vodafone Southern Zone Deans Competition, Nasinu Secondary School continued their dominant run across all gra

item.title: Portugal and Spain set for blockbuster World Cup last 16 clash
item.description: Portugal and Spain will renew their football rivalry in the next hour as they face off in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup Round of 16
